title: equisetum funstonii var. ramosum
intro: Equisetum funstonii var. ramosum, also known as Funston's horsetail, is a type of flowering plant in the horsetail family (Equisetaceae). This perennial plant is native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in silica, which makes it beneficial for skin and hair care, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis, skin irritations, and wounds. Herbal preparations of Funston's horsetail include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, and may also help to improve bone density and reduce the risk of osteoporosis.
